message:
feat(harness): add kernel regression report CLI and expand alpha_investing + perf infrastructure

Introduce a strict-vs-hardened kernel regression comparison tool and supporting
infrastructure for measuring runtime_math performance across modes.

Kernel regression report (new):
- crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/kernel_regression_report.rs: 611-line module that
  spawns two subprocesses (GLIBC_RUST_MODE=strict and =hardened) via
  KernelRegressionMode, collects per-mode JSON metrics (decision distributions,
  severity histograms, snapshot trends, microbench latency), and generates a
  markdown diff report highlighting behavioral deltas between modes
- harness.rs: add KernelRegressionReport and KernelRegressionMode CLI commands
  with configurable seed, steps, warmup_iters, samples, iters, and trend_stride
- harness lib.rs: register kernel_regression_report module

Alpha investing expansion:
- alpha_investing.rs: add 293 lines expanding the Foster & Stine (2008)
  sequential FDR controller with additional test vectors, cadence-gated
  meta-observation integration, and refined wealth/alpha budget accounting

Performance infrastructure:
- runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs: new criterion benchmark suite for runtime_math
  kernel hot-path latency (decision cycle, meta_observe, snapshot generation)
- Cargo.toml: add criterion benchmark harness dependency
- perf_gate.sh: add system overload detection (SKIP_OVERLOADED, MAX_LOAD_FACTOR
  via /proc/loadavg and nproc) to avoid false regressions on oversubscribed
  machines; add optional ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE toggle for per-kernel benchmarks
- update_golden_snapshots.sh: minor script maintenance updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -33,6 +33,10 @@ harness = false
 name = "runtime_math_bench"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "runtime_math_kernels_bench"
+harness = false
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "stdio_bench"
 harness = false

@@ -12,6 +12,12 @@ BASELINE_FILE="${BASELINE_FILE:-scripts/perf_baseline.json}"
 # Tighten locally by setting GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_REGRESSION_PCT.
 MAX_REGRESSION_PCT="${GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_REGRESSION_PCT:-15}"
 ALLOW_TARGET_VIOLATION="${GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ALLOW_TARGET_VIOLATION:-1}"
+# If the machine is heavily oversubscribed, nanosecond-scale regressions become
+# dominated by scheduler noise. Default: skip perf gating under extreme load.
+SKIP_OVERLOADED="${GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED:-1}"
+MAX_LOAD_FACTOR="${GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR:-0.85}"
+# Optional: run the per-kernel suite (can be enabled once baseline entries exist).
+ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE="${GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE:-0}"
 
 if [[ ! -f "${BASELINE_FILE}" ]]; then
   echo "perf_gate: missing baseline file: ${BASELINE_FILE}" >&2
@@ -23,6 +29,34 @@ if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   exit 2
 fi
 
+should_skip_overloaded() {
+  if [[ "${SKIP_OVERLOADED}" != "1" || ! -r /proc/loadavg ]]; then
+    return 1
+  fi
+  if ! command -v nproc >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    return 1
+  fi
+
+  local load1 cpus threshold overloaded
+  load1="$(awk '{print $1}' /proc/loadavg)"
+  cpus="$(nproc)"
+  threshold="$(awk -v c="${cpus}" -v f="${MAX_LOAD_FACTOR}" 'BEGIN { printf "%.2f", c*f }')"
+  overloaded="$(awk -v l="${load1}" -v t="${threshold}" 'BEGIN { print (l > t) ? 1 : 0 }')"
+
+  if [[ "${overloaded}" == "1" ]]; then
+    echo "perf_gate: SKIP (system overloaded) load1=${load1} cpus=${cpus} threshold=${threshold}"
+    echo "perf_gate: top CPU processes:"
+    ps -eo pid,user,comm,%cpu,etime --sort=-%cpu | head -n 10 || true
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  return 1
+}
+
+if should_skip_overloaded; then
+  exit 0
+fi
+
 extract_p50() {
   local prefix="$1" mode="$2" bench="$3"
   awk -v prefix="$prefix" -v mode="$mode" -v bench="$bench" '
@@ -71,11 +105,15 @@ check_metric() {
 
 run_mode() {
   local mode="$1"
-  local out_rt out_mem
+  local out_rt out_mem out_kernels
 
   echo ""
   echo "=== perf_gate: mode=${mode} ==="
 
+  if should_skip_overloaded; then
+    return 0
+  fi
+
   # Pin if supported by bench binaries (best-effort).
   out_rt="$(
     GLIBC_RUST_BENCH_PIN=1 GLIBC_RUST_MODE="$mode" \
@@ -89,6 +127,16 @@ run_mode() {
       | rg '^MEMBRANE_BENCH ' || true
   )"
 
+  if [[ "${ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE}" == "1" ]]; then
+    out_kernels="$(
+      GLIBC_RUST_BENCH_PIN=1 GLIBC_RUST_MODE="$mode" \
+        cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_kernels_bench 2>/dev/null \
+        | rg '^RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH ' || true
+    )"
+  else
+    out_kernels=""
+  fi
+
   if [[ -z "${out_rt}" ]]; then
     echo "perf_gate: failed to collect RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH lines for mode=${mode}" >&2
     exit 2
@@ -155,13 +203,23 @@ run_mode() {
     return 2
   fi
 
+  if [[ "${ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE}" == "1" ]]; then
+    if [[ -z "${out_kernels}" ]]; then
+      echo "perf_gate: kernel suite enabled but no RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines collected for mode=${mode}" >&2
+      return 1
+    fi
+    echo "perf_gate: kernel suite collected (mode=${mode}) lines=$(printf \"%s\\n\" \"${out_kernels}\" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
+  fi
+
   return 0
 }
 
 echo "=== perf_gate ==="
 echo "baseline=${BASELINE_FILE}"
 echo "max_regression_pct=${MAX_REGRESSION_PCT}"
 echo "allow_target_violation=${ALLOW_TARGET_VIOLATION}"
+echo "skip_overloaded=${SKIP_OVERLOADED} max_load_factor=${MAX_LOAD_FACTOR}"
+echo "enable_kernel_suite=${ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE}"
 
 run_mode strict
 run_mode hardened

@@ -2,7 +2,8 @@
 # Regenerate committed golden runtime_math snapshot fixtures + sha256sums.
 #
 # This is the intentional-update path when kernel snapshot behavior changes
-# and you have an isomorphism proof for the change.
+# and you have an isomorphism proof for the change (see:
+# scripts/POLICY_CHANGE_ISOMORPHISM_PROOF_TEMPLATE.md).
 set -euo pipefail
 
 ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
@@ -31,4 +32,3 @@ echo ""
 echo "update_golden_snapshots: updated:"
 echo "- ${SNAPSHOT_FILE}"
 echo "- ${SHA_FILE}"
-
